behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact." Slot B: decisive signal is module handoff (evaluate-stage command -> artifact store key selection). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real wiring bug where command-to-store mapping still targets `plan` would be missed. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is about artifact slot routing, not real OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here is storage destination, not model output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command with real in-process artifact repository and run context; assert a new/updated `grounding` artifact exists and `plan` is untouched by this stage. Use module wiring (command + repository + context), not a mocked write method.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`." Slot B: decisive signal is state handoff across local modules (artifact read path + artifact write path in one flow). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss a real failure where the orchestrator reads wrong artifact ID or writes output back to `grounding`. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text constrains data plumbing and destination artifact, not quality of model reasoning; rejected System because no real external infra is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with `grounding`, execute implement flow through real orchestrator and artifact repo, then assert implement consumed grounding input path and produced/updated `plan` output artifact without overwriting `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content." Slot B: decisive signal is module interaction between deep-dive stage gating logic and artifact writer side effects. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a regression in stage wiring that still calls the plan-writer despite gate conditions can pass isolated function tests. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because the required assertion is absence of `plan` overwrite side effect, explicitly tied to existing issue content; rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Execute deep-dive stage with issue content containing diagnosis in an integrated run context; verify deep-dive does not write/overwrite `plan` artifact while other expected stage outputs remain intact.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context." Slot B: decisive signal is cross-stage state handoff within shared run-context artifact lineage. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you may miss context-ID/lineage propagation bugs that only appear when stages are wired through the real repository abstraction. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text scopes to same run context lineage, not external infra behavior; rejected Workflow because only one handoff boundary is required to validate this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate/write stage to produce `grounding` in a real in-process run context, then invoke implement in same context and assert it resolves that exact lineage entry (same run/session identifiers) before writing `plan`.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier." Slot B: decisive signal is multi-step agentic sequence with dependence on earlier grounding in later plan generation. Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, you can miss failures where each step works in isolation but grounding is dropped/mutated across the chain, so final plan does not reflect earlier grounding. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires full multi-step run and reflection in final generated plan, which is sequenced agent behavior beyond single-boundary wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end chain with real agent stages in one run context and seeded issue; assert final `plan` contains concrete grounding-derived elements produced in earlier stage (traceable markers/claims), validating cross-step carryover.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts." Slot B: decisive signal is fallback orchestration plus multi-artifact state integrity across modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss repository-level corruption/regression where fallback path rewrites unrelated artifacts despite local logic appearing correct. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic because pass criteria are safe fallback and artifact integrity, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts, execute implement flow through real repository/orchestrator, then assert `plan` is produced, fallback path is taken, and pre-existing non-plan artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
